papers.title: PokeVLA: Empowering Pocket-Sized Vision-Language-Action Model with Comprehensive World Knowledge Guidance
papers.abstract: Recent advances in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have opened new avenues for robot manipulation, yet existing methods exhibit limited efficiency and a lack of high-level knowledge and spatial awareness. To address these challenges, we propose PokeVLA, a lightweight yet powerful foundation model for embodied manipulation that effectively infuses vision-language understanding into action learning.

papers.title: X-Cache: Cross-Chunk Block Caching for Few-Step Autoregressive World Models Inference
papers.abstract: Real-time world simulation for autonomous driving. Proposes cross-chunk block caching to accelerate autoregressive world model inference for driving simulation. Driving world models built on autoregressive video diffusion achieve high-fidelity controllable multi-camera generation but are bottlenecked by inference cost.

papers.title: Temporal Difference Calibration in Sequential Tasks: Application to Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: Temporal difference calibration method for VLA models in sequential tasks, improving temporal consistency of action generation.

papers.title: From Scene to Object: Text-Guided Dual-Gaze Prediction for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Interpretable driver attention prediction for human-like autonomous driving using VLM-based dual-gaze prediction with text-grounded object-level annotations.

papers.title: OVPD: A Virtual-Physical Fusion Testing Dataset of On-Site Autonomous Driving Challenge
papers.abstract: Testing dataset for autonomous driving fusing virtual simulation with real physical testing data, providing replayable and diagnosable scenarios.

papers.title: Toward Cooperative Driving in Mixed Traffic: An Adaptive Potential Game-Based Approach with Field Test Verification
papers.abstract: Connected autonomous vehicles cooperative decision-making using adaptive potential game theory with real field testing.

papers.title: SpanVLA: Efficient Action Bridging and Learning from Negative-Recovery Samples for Vision-Language-Action Model
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer a promising autonomous driving paradigm for leveraging world knowledge and reasoning capabilities, especially in long-tail scenarios. However, existing VLA models often struggle with high latency in action generation using autoregressive frameworks. SpanVLA proposes efficient action bridging and negative-recovery sample learning for end-to-end autonomous driving.

papers.title: Diffusion-Based Trajectory Planning with Safety Guarantees
papers.abstract: We develop a diffusion-based trajectory planning method with formal safety guarantees for autonomous driving. The approach combines diffusion models with control barrier functions to ensure safe navigation.

papers.title: VLA-Driver: Vision-Language-Action Pretraining for Urban Driving
papers.abstract: We introduce VLA-Driver, a vision-language-action pretraining framework specifically designed for urban driving scenarios. The model demonstrates strong zero-shot transfer capabilities across different driving datasets.

papers.title: LLM-Augmented World Models for Interpretable Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: We propose LLM-augmented world models that use large language models to provide interpretable explanations of driving decisions. The system improves transparency and trust in autonomous driving systems.

papers.title: UniAD 2.0: Unified Autonomous Driving with Multi-Task World Models
papers.abstract: We present UniAD 2.0, an enhanced unified autonomous driving framework that integrates multi-task world models for perception, prediction, and planning. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on nuScenes and Waymo Open Dataset benchmarks.

papers.title: BEV-Transformer v2: Scalable Bird's Eye View Perception with Adaptive Attention
papers.abstract: We propose BEV-Transformer v2, a scalable bird's eye view perception model with adaptive attention mechanisms. The model efficiently handles large-scale driving scenes with improved computational efficiency.

papers.title: ST-Prune: Training-Free Spatio-Temporal Token Pruning for Vision-Language Models in Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Training-free spatio-temporal token pruning method for VLMs in autonomous driving. Exploits inherent redundancies in multi-view camera and multi-frame video inputs to reduce computational overhead.

papers.title: PanDA: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Multimodal 3D Panoptic Segmentation in Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: First study on UDA for multimodal 3D panoptic segmentation in autonomous driving using pseudo-labeling strategies.

papers.title: Localization-Guided Foreground Augmentation in Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Foreground augmentation for autonomous driving under adverse visibility conditions using HD map priors.

papers.title: Diffusion Policy for Robust Autonomous Driving in Adverse Conditions
papers.abstract: We propose a diffusion-based policy network for autonomous driving that demonstrates robustness in adverse weather conditions. The model uses denoising diffusion process to generate safe trajectories.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Fusion for End-to-End Autonomous Driving with Uncertainty Estimation
papers.abstract: We propose a multi-modal fusion framework for end-to-end autonomous driving that incorporates uncertainty estimation to improve safety and reliability in complex driving scenarios.

papers.title: VLA-Drive: Vision-Language-Action Model with Dynamic Scene Graph Reasoning
papers.abstract: We propose VLA-Drive, a vision-language-action model that incorporates dynamic scene graph reasoning for better understanding of complex driving scenarios. The model can reason about object relationships and predict their future states.

papers.title: Physics-Informed World Models for Autonomous Driving Simulation
papers.abstract: We introduce physics-informed world models that incorporate physical constraints into the simulation process, enabling more realistic driving scenario generation for training autonomous driving systems.

papers.title: LLM-Agent for Interactive Autonomous Driving with Natural Language Commands
papers.abstract: We develop an LLM-based agent system for interactive autonomous driving that can understand and execute natural language commands from passengers. The system shows strong performance in human-vehicle interaction tasks.

papers.title: Unified World-Action Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving with Geometric Consistency
papers.abstract: We present a novel unified world-action model that integrates geometric consistency constraints into end-to-end autonomous driving. Our approach uses differentiable rendering to ensure physical plausibility of predicted trajectories and generates high-fidelity future scenes.

papers.title: BEVFormer++: Enhanced Bird's Eye View Perception with Temporal Fusion
papers.abstract: We present BEVFormer++, an enhanced version of BEVFormer that incorporates temporal fusion mechanisms for more accurate perception in autonomous driving. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on nuScenes benchmark.

papers.title: Asset Harvester: Extracting 3D Assets from Autonomous Driving Logs for Simulation
papers.abstract: Extracts 3D assets from AV driving logs for closed-loop simulation, enabling scalable testing and safety validation.

papers.title: Driving Risk Emerges from the Required Two-Dimensional Joint Evasive Acceleration
papers.abstract: Safety benchmark for autonomous driving modeling risk as 2D joint evasive acceleration instead of 1D TTC.

papers.title: OneVL: One-Step Latent Reasoning and Planning with Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning has become a powerful driver of trajectory prediction in VLA-based autonomous driving, yet its autoregressive nature imposes a latency cost that is prohibitive for real-time deployment. Latent CoT methods attempt to close this gap by compressing reasoning into continuous hidden states, but consistently fall short of their explicit counterparts. We suggest that ...

papers.title: OneDrive: Unified Multi-Paradigm Driving with Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: Vision-Language Models(VLMs) excel at autoregressive text generation, yet end-to-end autonomous driving requires multi-task learning with structured outputs and heterogeneous decoding behaviors, such as autoregressive language generation, parallel object detection and trajectory regression. To accommodate these differences, existing systems typically introduce separate or cascaded decoders ...

papers.title: POMDP-based Object Search with Growing State Space and Hybrid Action Domain
papers.abstract: Efficiently locating target objects in complex indoor environments with diverse furniture, such as shelves, tables, and beds, is a significant challenge for mobile robots...

papers.title: World-Value-Action Model: Implicit Planning for Vision-Language-Action Systems
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising paradigm for building embodied agents that ground perception and language into action...

papers.title: AD4AD: Benchmarking Visual Anomaly Detection Models for Safer Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Benchmarks visual anomaly detection models for autonomous driving, addressing out-of-distribution obstacle detection.

papers.title: CooperDrive: Enhancing Driving Decisions Through Cooperative Perception
papers.abstract: Cooperative perception framework augmenting situational awareness in occlusion/NLOS scenarios for autonomous vehicles.

papers.title: FeaXDrive: Feasibility-aware Trajectory-Centric Diffusion Planning for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: End-to-end diffusion planning for autonomous driving addressing physical feasibility of generated trajectories. Proposes feasibility-aware trajectory-centric formulation that enforces kinematic constraints and geometric regularity in the diffusion process.

papers.title: Unveiling the Surprising Efficacy of Navigation Understanding in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Analyzes how end-to-end driving systems rely on global navigation vs local scene understanding. Shows many E2E models over-rely on local understanding and fail to utilize global navigation information, revealing weak correlation between planning capabilities and navigation understanding.

papers.title: MVAdapt: Zero-Shot Multi-Vehicle Adaptation for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: E2E driving models degrade substantially when deployed on vehicles with different dynamics (size, mass, drivetrain). Proposes zero-shot multi-vehicle adaptation addressing this vehicle-domain gap without retraining.

papers.title: Multi-ORFT: Stable Online Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent Diffusion Planning in Cooperative Driving
papers.abstract: Stable online RL fine-tuning for multi-agent diffusion planners in cooperative driving scenarios with closed-loop evaluation.

papers.title: BridgeSim: Unveiling the OL-CL Gap in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Reveals root causes of open-loop to closed-loop gap in E2E driving policies: Observational Domain Shift and Objective Mismatch. Proposes practical remedy bridging the OL-CL gap.

papers.title: Scaling-Aware Data Selection for End-to-End Autonomous Driving Systems
papers.abstract: Large-scale deep learning models for physical AI applications depend on diverse training data collection efforts...

papers.title: DriveDreamer-Policy: A Geometry-Grounded World-Action Model for Unified Generation and Planning
papers.abstract: Recently, world-action models (WAM) have emerged to bridge vision-language-action (VLA) models and world models...

papers.title: DLWM: Dual Latent World Models enable Holistic Gaussian-centric Pre-training in Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Vision-based autonomous driving has gained much attention due to its low costs and excellent performance...
